user_claim: Customer: I am opening a claim for my windshield. | Support: What happened? | Customer: A small stone hit it while I was driving and now there is a crack spreading from that spot. | Support: Is the car otherwise okay? | Customer: Yes, this is only about the front glass. I added the pictures I have.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The submitted images contradict each other regarding the claimed damage. Image img_1 shows a severely damaged windshield, while image img_2 shows an undamaged windshield on what appears to be the same vehicle.
claim_status_justification: Image img_1 clearly depicts a windshield with extensive crack and shatter damage. However, image img_2, showing the same part of the car, presents an entirely undamaged windshield. This direct contradiction between the visual evidence prevents a conclusive assessment of the claim.

user_claim: Customer: Someone clipped my car while it was parked. | Support: What part was hit? | Customer: The side mirror got damaged. It is not sitting the way it should anymore. | Support: Was there any damage to the door too? | Customer: I did not notice anything else major, just the mirror.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image clearly shows the claimed side mirror and the damage to its glass.
claim_status_justification: Image img_1 clearly shows the side mirror of a red car with its glass severely shattered. This supports the claim of a damaged side mirror.

user_claim: Customer: I want to file this as bumper damage. | Support: Can you tell me what happened? | Customer: The car was tapped from behind and now the back looks damaged. | Support: Are you asking for review of the rear side? | Customer: Yes, the back bumper. evidence_standard_met_reason: The images clearly show the rear of the vehicle, including the bumper and adjacent body panels, allowing for assessment of the claimed damage.
claim_status_justification: Image img_2 provides a clear view of the rear bumper, which appears to be undamaged. While img_1 shows dents, these are located on the rear quarter panel, not the bumper as claimed.

claim_status_justification: The provided image (img_1) is a generic stock photo of a car on a road and does not show the specific car or the headlight claimed to be cracked. No damage is visible.

user_claim: Customer: There is a dent along the side of my car. | Support: When did you notice it? | Customer: This morning, after the car had been parked near the gate. | Support: Is it near the front or back? | Customer: It is on the door panel area. evidence_standard_met_reason: Image img_2 clearly shows a dent on the car's door panel, supporting the claim.
claim_status_justification: Image img_2 clearly shows a significant dent on the lower part of the car's door panel, consistent with the customer's claim. Image img_1 is too blurry to assess any damage.

user_claim: Customer: I picked up my car after service and noticed a mark on the hood. | Support: What kind of mark is it? | Customer: It looks like a scratch across the top panel. | Support: Do you think it happened during service? | Customer: Yes, that is why I am asking for review. evidence_standard_met_reason: The submitted image (img_1) is a stock photo with 'Vecteezy' watermarks, indicating it is not an original photo taken by the user. It also depicts severe, extensive damage to the front of a car, which is inconsistent with the claim of a simple scratch on the hood.
claim_status_justification: The claim cannot be assessed because the submitted image (img_1) is a watermarked stock photo and does not provide valid evidence. The image also shows severe damage to the car's front end, which contradicts the claim of a scratch on the hood.
